project.title: Proteomic Analysis of intersection protein that binds to KDM1A in Rat primary cardiomyocytes
project.projectDescription: The intersection proteins under hypoxia condition (0, 24, 48 h) in primary neonatal rat ventricular myocytes (NRVMs) were detected by Mass spectrometry.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Cardiomyocytes (NRVMs) were infected with lentiviruses carrying the Flag-KDM1A plasmid, and cell lysates were immunoprecipitated using anti-Flag affinity magnetic beads. Immunoprecipitated proteins were subjected to protein electrophoresis, followed by Coomassie blue staining. Then the protein bands were excised and analyzed using mass spectrometry. Full-scan mass spectrometry spectra of the protein samples were obtained at a resolution of 60,000 at 200 m/z with a maximum injection time of 20 ms. The 15 most intense ions were fragmented using high collision-induced dissociation. The resolution was set to 15,000 at 200 m/z, AGC target to 1E5, and maximum injection time to 25 ms.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: PI software was used to find mass spectrometry data of theoretical simulation framengatation, then the corresponding protein was extracted. Input the experimental mass spectrometry data files of specific format to handle, and score the similarity between experimental data and theoretical data, and give the identification result.

references.referenceLine: Su Q, Qin JQ, Huang Y, Dai RX, Wu QY, Mo LR, Wu Q, Huang WZ, Yang HF, Liu YC, Pan DG. USP16 S-nitrosylation aggravates coronary microembolization-induced myocardial injury via repressing KDM1A-mediated glutathione homeostasis. Nat Commun. 2025 17(1):255

Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: cell lysates were immunoprecipitated using anti-Flag affinity magnetic beads.
Comment[Instrument]: A mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) was operated in data-dependent mode (MS scans, 300–1650 m/z).
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: The 15 most intense ions were fragmented using high collision-induced dissociation.
FactorValue[Treatment]: Control cells were cultured under normal conditions (21% O2/5% CO2).

Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: cell lysates were immunoprecipitated using anti-Flag affinity magnetic beads.
Comment[Instrument]: A mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) was operated in data-dependent mode (MS scans, 300–1650 m/z).
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: The 15 most intense ions were fragmented using high collision-induced dissociation.
FactorValue[Treatment]: exposed to hypoxic conditions (1% O2/5% CO2) for 12, 24, or 48 h to simulate the hypoxic microenvironment during CME in vitro.
